title: Warm Analog Bass with FLEX
goal: Create a rounded, mono-safe analog-style bass suitable for trap.

steps.action: Load a subtractive bass preset and keep it dry — no reverb or delay on the low end.
steps.tip: Lower a brightness macro for a darker tone; illustrative cutoff around 30-40%.

steps.action: Shorten the envelope Release (~5-10%) so notes stay tight and do not overlap.
steps.tip: Check the bass in mono; wide bass loses power on club systems.

steps.action: High-pass rumble below the fundamental (Band 1) and gently lift the body around 100-200Hz.

title: Vocal Clarity with Fruity Parametric EQ 2
goal: Clean up and open a lead vocal without making it harsh.

steps.action: High-pass with Band 1 around 80Hz to remove rumble and free up headroom.
steps.tip: Never boost Band 1 unless you are designing bass.

steps.action: Cut low-mid mud around 200-300Hz (Band 2/3) with a moderate bandwidth.
steps.tip: Bandwidth is inverted here: lower % = wider.

steps.action: Add a gentle presence lift near 2-3kHz (Band 5) for intelligibility.
steps.tip: Presence stacks across tracks — overuse causes harshness.

steps.action: Optionally add a high-shelf 'air' around 10kHz (Band 7) for modern gloss.

title: FM Sub Bass with Sytrus
goal: Design a tuned, harmonically rich sub bass using FM.

steps.action: Start from a simple algorithm with one operator modulating a near-sine carrier.
steps.tip: Keep operator frequency in Ratio mode so the pitch stays tuned.

steps.action: Raise modulation amount gradually to add harmonics; keep the envelope attack near-instant and release short.
steps.tip: Small modulation changes shift timbre a lot — move slowly.

steps.action: High-pass sub-rumble and tame any harsh upper harmonic with a narrow cut.
